pi_items.source: draw.io documentation (https://docs.fileformat.com/web/drawio/) and task description. .drawio is the standard XML-based file format for diagrams.net/draw.io files.
setup_task.sh line 13 and export_result.sh line 10 explicitly check for file at /home/ga/Desktop/login_flowchart.drawio. env.json configures user 'ga' with Desktop available.

task.json description explicitly specifies 7 labeled shapes: 1) Start (ellipse), 2) Enter Username (rectangle), 3) Enter Password (rectangle), 4) Valid? (diamond), 5) Show Error (rectangle), 6) Login Success (rectangle), 7) End (ellipse).

task.json description states 'minimum 6 arrows' and lists required connections: Start→Username, Username→Password, Password→Valid, Valid→Success, Valid→Error, Success→End. verifier.py line 168 confirms minimum of 3 connections for pass, but metadata specifies 6 as minimum.

ISO 5807:1985 flowchart standard (https://www.iso.org/standard/11955.html). Verified in draw.io documentation (https://www.drawio.com/doc/getting-started-basic-flow-chart) which states: Terminal/Terminator shapes = Ellipse (Start/End), Process = Rectangle, Decision = Diamond. These are standard international flowchart symbols.

privileged_info_summary: This task uses the real draw.io/diagrams.net application to create a login process flowchart. The file format is .drawio, an XML-based format developed by diagrams.net. The task requires exactly 7 labeled shapes using standard ISO 5807 flowchart symbols: 2 terminal shapes (ellipses for Start/End), 4 process shapes (rectangles for Enter Username, Enter Password, Show Error, Login Success), and 1 decision shape (diamond for Valid?). The diagram must have at least 6 arrows connecting these shapes. The file must be saved as 'login_flowchart.drawio' in the /home/ga/Desktop directory. All terminology and requirements are based on international standards for flowchart design.
